_comment: Source: FBI/CISA/NSA/NCSC joint advisory AA26-097A (April 2026) — Operation Masquerade / FrostArmada; CISA advisory AA24-038A (Feb 2024) — Operation Dying Ember / Moobot. Threat actor: APT28 (GRU Unit 26165 / Fancy Bear).
attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated HTTP GET to /tmp/dropbear/dropbearpwd retrieves stored plaintext credentials; second crafted POST to /userRpm/WanDynamicIpV6CfgRpm.htm modifies DNS server settings to APT28-controlled resolvers for DNS hijacking
affected_firmware: All versions prior to 220905 (Sept 2022 patch); TL-WR841N v14 and earlier confirmed exploited
remediation: Update firmware to latest available release, change all default credentials, disable WAN-side remote management (TCP 80/443), segment IoT/router admin interface from LAN, verify DNS settings have not been modified

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction from /tmp/dropbear/dropbearpwd followed by DNS hijack via crafted HTTP request to router management interface
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; v2 and v3 hardware confirmed vulnerable
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction followed by DNS hijack via crafted HTTP request
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; v5 hardware confirmed vulnerable
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction followed by DNS hijack via crafted HTTP request
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction followed by DNS hijack via crafted HTTP request
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; v1 hardware confirmed vulnerable
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management

affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; dual-band N300 platform affected

affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; dual-band N600 platform affected

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction followed by DNS hijack
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; dual-band N750 platform, v1 hardware confirmed
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management

http_title: TP-LINK Wireless N Gigabit Router WDR4300

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80/8080 — unauthenticated credential extraction; additionally CVE-2021-41753 allows command injection via the ping diagnostic feature (requires authentication but default creds trivially obtained)
affected_firmware: v2 hardware, all firmware prior to 150427; AC1200 dual-band Archer platform
remediation: Update firmware to latest available, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management, restrict diagnostic functions

attack_vector: Multiple vectors: (1) CVE-2023-50224 auth bypass + credential extraction on TCP 80; (2) CVE-2020-9374 unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted request to tdpServer on UDP 20002 (TP-Link Device Protocol); (3) CVE-2021-41753 command injection post-auth in diagnostic functions
affected_firmware: v2/v4/v5 hardware; AC1750 dual-band; all firmware prior to 200904 for v5
remediation: Update firmware to latest available, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management, block UDP 20002 externally

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction followed by DNS hijack; LTE/4G router with WAN-side management typically enabled by default
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; 4G LTE router, v4/v5 hardware
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN/LTE management interface, restrict admin access to LAN only

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction followed by DNS hijack; 3G/4G USB modem router
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; v5 hardware confirmed vulnerable
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, disable remote WAN management

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction; wireless access point used as pivot point after compromise for lateral movement onto wired LAN segments
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; v5 hardware, 300Mbps wireless N access point
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, restrict management VLAN access

http_title: TP-LINK Wireless N Access Point WA801ND

attack_vector: Authentication bypass in TP-Link HTTPD on TCP 80 — unauthenticated credential extraction; wireless N450 access point used as pivot point
affected_firmware: All versions prior to security patch; v5/v6 hardware confirmed
remediation: Update firmware, change default credentials, restrict management VLAN access

http_title: TP-LINK Wireless N Access Point WA901ND

threat_actor: APT28 / GRU Unit 26165 (Fancy Bear) — used Moobot botnet as pre-compromised infrastructure for credential harvesting and spearphishing operations
attack_vector: CVE-2023-31998: heap-based buffer overflow in EdgeOS multicast daemon (UDP) allows unauthenticated RCE as root. CVE-2021-22909: command injection via crafted upgrade URL in EdgeOS firmware update mechanism. Post-compromise: APT28 installs OpenSSH backdoor, exfiltrates NTLM hashes and browser credentials, establishes Moobot C2 tunnel via non-standard SSH port
affected_firmware: EdgeOS v2.0.9 and below (CVE-2023-31998 patched in v2.0.9-hotfix.4); all v1.x and v2.x before patch
remediation: Upgrade EdgeOS to v2.0.9-hotfix.4 or later; change all default credentials (default is ubnt/ubnt); disable WAN-side SSH (TCP 22) and HTTP (TCP 80); enable firewall rules blocking management access from WAN; check /etc/cron.d/ and /etc/rc.local for persistence mechanisms; audit authorized_keys in /home/ubnt/.ssh/

attack_vector: Same as EdgeRouter X: heap overflow in multicast daemon (CVE-2023-31998) for unauthenticated RCE; post-compromise credential harvesting and Moobot C2 tunneling
affected_firmware: EdgeOS v2.0.9 and below; EdgeRouter Lite runs identical EdgeOS firmware
remediation: Upgrade EdgeOS, change default ubnt/ubnt credentials, disable WAN-side management, audit for IOCs listed in CISA AA24-038A

attack_vector: Same as EdgeRouter X — heap overflow in multicast daemon for unauthenticated RCE; ER-4's quad-port design made it attractive for network edge compromise
affected_firmware: EdgeOS v2.0.9 and below
remediation: Upgrade EdgeOS, change default credentials, disable WAN management

attack_vector: Same as EdgeRouter X — heap overflow in multicast daemon for unauthenticated RCE; ER-6P's PoE ports made it valuable for compromising IP cameras and phones in addition to network routing
affected_firmware: EdgeOS v2.0.9 and below
remediation: Upgrade EdgeOS, change default credentials, disable WAN management, disable unnecessary PoE outputs

attack_vector: Same as EdgeRouter X — heap overflow in multicast daemon for unauthenticated RCE; ER-12's 12-port rack-mounted form factor targeted in small business/SMB environments
affected_firmware: EdgeOS v2.0.9 and below
remediation: Upgrade EdgeOS, change default ubnt/ubnt credentials, disable WAN management, review firewall rules

campaign: FrostArmada / Operation Masquerade — Ukrainian infrastructure targeting
attack_vector: CVE-2018-14847: Winbox (TCP 8291) authentication bypass allows unauthenticated read of /flash/rw/store/user.dat credential file containing MD5-like password hashes. CVE-2023-30799: privilege escalation from admin to 'super-admin' root in RouterOS via HTTP API — unauthenticated on versions prior to 6.49.8/7.10beta8 when HTTP/Winbox accessible. Combined: extract credentials then escalate to full root access for persistent implant installation
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8; 7.x prior to 7.9.2; hAP ac and hAP ac2 both confirmed exploited
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS to 6.49.8+ or 7.9.2+; disable Winbox (TCP 8291) from WAN interface; disable HTTP API from WAN; change default admin credentials (default is admin with empty password); review /ip firewall filter for unexpected rules; check /system scheduler for malicious cron entries

campaign: FrostArmada / Operation Masquerade — Ukrainian infrastructure targeting
attack_vector: Same as hAP ac: Winbox auth bypass (CVE-2018-14847) for credential extraction + CVE-2023-30799 privilege escalation to root
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8; 7.x prior to 7.9.2
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS, disable Winbox from WAN, change default empty-password admin credential

attack_vector: Same vector as hAP ac via Winbox protocol and HTTP API on TCP 8291/8728
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS, disable Winbox from WAN, change default empty-password admin credential

campaign: FrostArmada / Operation Masquerade — Ukrainian infrastructure targeting
attack_vector: Winbox (TCP 8291) credential extraction via CVE-2018-14847; RB750/RB750r2/RB750G series widely deployed as SOHO routers across Eastern Europe making them high-value targets; APT28 uses compromised units as proxy nodes within FrostArmada botnet
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS, block Winbox TCP 8291 from WAN, change admin credentials

campaign: FrostArmada / Operation Masquerade — Ukrainian infrastructure targeting
attack_vector: Same as RB750: Winbox authentication bypass for credential theft, used as botnet proxy node in FrostArmada campaign infrastructure
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8; RB951Ui-2nD (hAP) and RB951G-2HnD both affected
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS, block Winbox from WAN, change default credentials

attack_vector: Winbox CVE-2018-14847 credential extraction; RB2011 series is enterprise/ISP-grade with 10-port switching — compromise enables network-wide lateral movement; APT28 uses RB2011 units compromised via Winbox as persistent proxy relay nodes for C2 traffic obfuscation
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8; RB2011UiAS-2HnD-IN confirmed affected
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS to 6.49.8+, disable Winbox and HTTP API on WAN interfaces, change admin credentials, review ip/firewall/filter for persistence

campaign: FrostArmada / Operation Masquerade — high-bandwidth proxy infrastructure
attack_vector: Winbox CVE-2018-14847 + CVE-2023-30799 root escalation; CCR1009 tile-processor architecture provides ~12 Gbps routing throughput making compromised units high-value relay nodes for large-scale data exfiltration and C2 proxy infrastructure in FrostArmada network
affected_firmware: RouterOS 6.x prior to 6.49.8; CCR1009-7G-1C-1S+ and CCR1009-8G-1S-1S+ confirmed in advisory
remediation: Upgrade RouterOS to 6.49.8+, disable all management ports from WAN, change credentials, inspect /file/ for implanted scripts, check /system/script for malicious entries
